free_text: Measurement of neutrons from thick Fe target bombarded
- Physical-form of target is solid. Covered with
aluminium foil to absorb secondary electorons
- Target-thickness: 24 mm diameter by 55 mm thickness,
which can stop the incident protons completely
trigger signal from the cyclotron was used as
the stop signal for ToF measurement). Energy
resolution of ToF experiment is shown in Fig.6
as a function of neutron energy.
NE213 liquid scintillator used as E-counter and
ToF start signal for neutron energy
convert light output into MeV electron
NE102A liquid scintillator used as Delta E
Uncertainty in the calculated detection efficiency is
from 4% to 6% in the energy region below 38.3 MeV, and
within 10% in the energy region above 38.3 MeV.

current measured with a current integrator

correction factor in terms of absorption and
